questions.question: How can you create a code block on a Notion page?

options.text: Type `/code` and press Enter
options.explanation: According to the article, you can either click the `+` that appears on a new line and choose Code, or type `/code` and press Enter.

options.text: Click the Code icon in the formatting toolbar
options.explanation: The article does not mention a Code icon in the main formatting toolbar. It specifies using the `+` menu or the `/code` command.

options.text: Select the text and choose 'Code' from the text menu
options.explanation: Code blocks are created as new blocks, not by formatting existing text.

options.text: Press Ctrl+Shift+C to insert a block
options.explanation: This is not a standard Notion keyboard shortcut mentioned for creating code blocks.

questions.question: What is the primary purpose of adding a code block to a page?

options.text: To display code broken out from the rest of your content and formatted by language
options.explanation: The article introduction states that code blocks display code so it is 'broken out from the rest of your content and formatted by language'.

options.text: To create a new database within the page
options.explanation: Databases are created separately and not through the code block creation process.

options.text: To embed a video from YouTube
options.explanation: The article mentions a YouTube video in the sidebar, but that is for a different feature; code blocks are for text/code.

options.text: To hide sensitive information from other users
options.explanation: Code blocks do not inherently hide content; permissions control visibility.

questions.question: How do you change the syntax highlighting language for a code block?

options.text: Click the language name at the top left of the code block, type the language name, and press Enter
options.explanation: The article outlines two steps: clicking the language name at the top left and typing the language to search/select it.

options.text: Select the code and choose the language from the formatting menu
options.explanation: Syntax highlighting is set at the block level, not by selecting individual text.

options.text: Go to Settings > Appearance > Code Fonts
options.explanation: There is no mention of global settings for code font in this specific article.

options.text: You cannot change the syntax highlighting once the block is created
options.explanation: The article explicitly provides instructions on how to change the language display.

questions.question: What is the specific goal of the 'Wrap code' option?

options.text: To create a line break at the edge of the code block to eliminate side scrolling
options.explanation: The article states that Wrap code 'creates a line break at the edge of your code block' to 'eliminate side scrolling'.

options.text: To change the background color of the code block
options.explanation: Wrap code changes the text wrapping behavior, not the background color.

options.text: To allow the code block to be copied by users with only 'Can view' permission
options.explanation: Permission settings for Copy/Caption are separate from the Wrap code option.

options.text: To automatically format the code into lowercase
options.explanation: Syntax highlighting handles code formatting, not the Wrap code setting.

questions.question: Where do you find the 'Wrap code' setting to toggle it on or off?

options.text: Hover over the code block and click the `•••` menu at the top right (or `⋮⋮` on the left)
options.explanation: The instructions state to hover over the block and click the `•••` menu at the top right or the `⋮⋮` icon on the left.

options.text: Click the 'Copy' button that appears on hover
options.explanation: The Copy button is for copying code to the clipboard, not for toggling settings.

options.text: Right-click anywhere on the page background
options.explanation: You must right-click specifically on the code block itself to access the menu.

options.text: It is always toggled on by default and cannot be changed
options.explanation: Wrap code is a toggleable setting that must be manually switched on.

questions.question: What functionality does the 'Copy' button provide?

options.text: It copies the caption text to your clipboard
options.explanation: The Copy button copies the code itself, not the caption text.

options.text: It copies the code to your editor or wherever you want to share it
options.explanation: The article notes that clicking Copy prepares the code to be pasted into your editor.

options.text: It copies the image associated with the code block
options.explanation: Code blocks typically do not contain images; the Copy button acts on the text content.

options.text: It copies the page link to the code block
options.explanation: This is not a standard feature of the Copy button in this context.

questions.question: Which button allows you to add a descriptive text (caption) to a code block?

options.text: Select the 'Caption' button at the top right
options.explanation: The article explicitly states to 'Select Caption and enter whatever you want to say about the code!'

options.text: Click the '•••' menu and choose 'Add Caption'
options.explanation: While the menu has options, the primary and direct way to add a caption is via the Caption button.

options.text: Type `# Caption:` at the beginning of the code
options.explanation: This syntax is not supported for captions in Notion code blocks.

options.text: The 'Copy' button acts as the caption button
options.explanation: The Copy and Caption buttons are distinct buttons revealed on hover.

questions.question: Who can see the 'Copy' and 'Caption' buttons on a code block?

options.text: Users with 'Can view' permission levels
options.explanation: The Note at the end states these buttons are 'invisible to users who have 'Can view' permission levels'.

options.text: Users with 'Can comment' permission levels
options.explanation: While Can comment is a permission, the specific note regarding these buttons is about Can view.

options.text: Only the owner of the page
options.explanation: Permission restrictions are based on the 'Can view' level, not specific to the owner.

options.text: All users with access to the page
options.explanation: The specific group that cannot see them is users with 'Can view' permission.

questions.question: What visual indicators appear on a code block when you hover over it?

options.text: The 'Copy' and 'Caption' buttons at the top right
options.explanation: The article mentions that hovering reveals these two specific buttons.

options.text: A '•••' menu at the top right and a language selector at the top left
options.explanation: The `•••` menu is accessed by clicking the icon, not just by hovering, though the language selector is always visible.

options.text: The 'Wrap code' toggle switch
options.explanation: The Wrap code toggle is accessed by clicking the menu, not by hovering.

options.text: No buttons appear until you click inside the block
options.explanation: The Copy and Caption buttons appear on hover, not upon clicking inside.
